affecting Dallas County.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHEN...Until late this evening.

creek. Ball fields at Moss Park are completely flooded.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 6:15 AM CD
items.area: Dallas, TX

items.title: Tornado Warning issued April 4 at 6:22AM CDT until April 4 at 7:00AM CDT by NWS St Louis MO
items.summary:
TORLSX

The National Weather Service in St Louis has issued a

* Tornado Warning for...
Northeastern Randolph County in southwestern Illinois...

* Until 700 AM CDT.

* At 622 AM CDT, a severe thunderstorm capable of producing a tornado
was located over Evansville, or 9 miles northeast of Ste.
Genevieve, moving northeast at 35 mph.

HAZARD...Tornado.

SOURCE...Radar indicated rotation.

IMPACT...Flying debris will be dangerous to those caught without
shelter. Mobile homes will be damaged or dest
items.area: Randolph, IL

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued April 4 at 6:18AM CDT by NWS Fort Worth TX
items.summary:
At 618 AM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking strong thunderstorms along
a line extending from near Celina to Carrollton. Movement was east at
20 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects.

Strong thunderstorms will be near...
Dallas, Plano, McKinney, Carrollton, Frisco, Allen, The Colony,
Addison, Prosper, and Celina around 625 AM CDT.
Richardson around 630 AM CDT.
Anna and Melissa around 63
items.area: Denton; Collin

items.title: Rip Current Statement issued April 4 at 5:57AM CDT until April 4 at 7:00PM CDT by NWS Houston/Galveston TX
items.summary:
* WHAT...Dangerous rip currents.

* WHERE...Gulf-facing beaches, including the Matagorda
Peninsula, Brazoria County beaches, Galveston Island and the
Bolivar Peninsula.

* WHEN...Through Saturday evening.

* IMPACTS...Rip currents can sweep even the best swimmers away
from shore into deeper water.

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued April 4 at 6:00AM EDT by NWS Wilmington NC
items.summary:
Patchy dense fog will occur across the area this morning.
Motorists encountering the fog will see visibility quickly reduce
to less than 1/2 mile at times making driving hazardous at times.
The fog is expected to quickly burn off between 8 and 9 am this
morning.
items.area: Robeson; Marlboro; Darlington; Dillon; Florence

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued April 4 at 5:51AM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
items.summary:
Patchy dense fog is develop across the portions of the Suwannee
River Valley and across portions of Interstate 10 this morning and
will affect these areas until 9 AM. Given the nature of the fog
visibilities on area roadways can reduce to under one half mile or
less over a short distances. Smoke produced by area wildfires
could enhance fog and result in very low visibilities.
items.area: Baker; Union; Bradford; Eastern Hamilton; Northern Columbia; Southeastern Columbia; Southwestern Columbia; Echols; Clinch; Southern Ware; Western Charlton

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 4 at 4:48AM CDT until April 4 at 7:00PM CDT by NWS Grand Forks ND
items.summary:
* WHAT...Snow. Additional snow accumulations up to two inches.

* WHERE...Portions of northwest Minnesota and northeast North Dakota.

* WHEN...Until 7 PM CDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Plan on slippery road conditions. Mainly drifting snow
is expected, but in areas prone to blowing snow, the snow may be
lofted and briefly reduce visibility. The combination of warm
temperatures and drifting snow can cause snow to adhere to roads,
creating icy patches. The hazardous conditions will impact the

items.summary:
* WHAT...Heavy snow expected. Total snow accumulations between 3 and
9 inches.

Forks and Nelson Counties.

items.summary:
* WHAT...Heavy snow and patchy blowing snow. Additional snow
accumulations between 4 and 6 inches.

* WHERE...Grant County.

* WHEN...Until 7 PM CDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Plan on slippery road conditions. The combination of
falling snow and winds will lead to periods of blowing snow that

items.summary:
* WHAT...West winds 10 to 15 kt with gusts up to 25 kt expected.
Waves up to 2 ft.

* WHERE...Southern Bay of Green Bay.

* IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
